differential_diagnoses: Lemierre's syndrome (variant with right renal vein thrombosis/duodenal involvement)
Penetrating duodenal ulcer with duodenorenal fistula and secondary septic emboli
next_steps: Perform transesophageal echocardiography to rule out endocarditis
Consult interventional radiology for image-guided drainage of the suspected periduodenal/pararenal collection
Obtain follow-up imaging (repeat CT or MRI with better technique) to characterize the duodenorenal connection
Evaluate for metastatic abscesses (liver, mediastinum, or retroperitoneum)
Maintain therapeutic anticoagulation while monitoring for bleeding risk
Escalate to broad-spectrum antibiotic therapy (e.g., ceftriaxone and metronidazole or piperacillin-tazobactam) for complex anaerobic/Streptococcus anginosus infections
